findings.description: Attachment source list formatting changed from plain lines to bulleted list.
findings.evidence: On page 3, the PDF attachment lists funding sources as plain left-aligned lines, while the HTML renders them as bullet points. The content and order appear preserved, but the visual formatting differs.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy in the HTML does not accurately reflect the document structure and uses multiple h1 elements where the PDF presents section titles rather than multiple document titles.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

Part A. Expenditures and Provision Method

and later another

Part B. Recipients

. It also uses

SOCIAL SERVICES BLOCK GRANT (SSBG) POST-EXPENDITURE REPORT

followed by

North Carolina Annual Report of Services Funded with SSBG, SFY 2014

. In the PDF, these are page/section titles, not a hierarchy with multiple top-level document titles.

findings.description: There is a text accuracy error in the report period field; the HTML changes the start year from 07/13 to 07/13 while the PDF appears to show 07/13 to 06/14 as printed, but the generated field formatting is potentially inaccurate and nonstandard compared with the source form's exact presentation.
findings.evidence: HTML states: 'REPORT PERIOD: 07/13 to 06/14'. The PDF top-right field also reads 'REPORT PERIOD: 07/13 to 06/14', but the HTML normalizes the layout and may not preserve the exact form-field presentation. No stronger substantive text discrepancy was confirmed elsewhere.
